<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Including architecture-policy.test.js in the E2E suite rather than a separate lint step means policy violations surface in the same test run as functional regressions, reducing context switching.
[observation] [info] With 14 files and the list truncated at 8, confirm the remaining 6 files do not duplicate coverage already present in the listed tests — redundant E2E tests are expensive to maintain without proportional safety gains.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling to all other zones is the ideal state for an E2E suite — tests drive the CLI as an external consumer and never import from application source, keeping the suite honest.
[pattern] [warning] The e2e suite covers CLI orchestration but has no counterpart suite for the HTTP/MCP surface exposed by the web package — this is a coverage gap for the server transport layer that grows in importance as the HTTP MCP transport becomes the recommended integration path
[relationship] [info] cli-e2e-tests is the sole zone that exercises cross-package behavior end-to-end; its zero-coupling guarantee means any regression in inter-package contracts (e.g. rex ↔ hench, sourcevision ↔ rex) will only surface here, not in unit or integration suites — preserve this zone's isolation strictly
[anti-pattern] [warning] The 6 files omitted from the zone listing cannot be audited for the zero-coupling invariant that gives this suite its architectural value. Confirm the unlisted files do not import from application source to preserve the black-box guarantee.
[suggestion] [info] Add a comment block or README in tests/e2e/ documenting that e2e tests are intentionally plain .js (no build step) so contributors do not convert them to .ts and inadvertently require transpilation in the test runner.
[suggestion] [info] Rename architecture-policy.test.js to a name that does not imply CLI command scope (e.g. monorepo-policy.test.js or architecture-invariants.test.js) to preserve the 'cli-' prefix convention across the suite.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm this is a fully self-contained test suite with no accidental dependency on application internals
- The architecture-policy test is a valuable meta-guard — it enforces structural invariants (e.g. no circular deps) as part of the CI gate rather than leaving them to convention
- 14 test files covering distinct CLI commands suggests good command-level isolation; verify that shared test helpers are not duplicated across files and are extracted to a fixture or helper module
- Zero coupling to all other zones is the ideal state for an E2E suite — tests drive the CLI as an external consumer and never import from application source, keeping the suite honest.
- Including architecture-policy.test.js in the E2E suite rather than a separate lint step means policy violations surface in the same test run as functional regressions, reducing context switching.
- With 14 files and the list truncated at 8, confirm the remaining 6 files do not duplicate coverage already present in the listed tests — redundant E2E tests are expensive to maintain without proportional safety gains.
- The cli-e2e-tests zone sits at the monorepo root and exercises the orchestration CLI, making it the only test zone that indirectly validates the full four-tier dependency hierarchy (orchestration → execution → domain → foundation) through the CLI surface — it is the integration safety net for cross-package contracts
- No e2e test zone exists for the web package's HTTP surface (MCP endpoints, dashboard routes) — the cli-e2e-tests cover CLI delegation but leave the web server's externally-facing HTTP API untested at the e2e level
- The e2e suite covers CLI orchestration but has no counterpart suite for the HTTP/MCP surface exposed by the web package — this is a coverage gap for the server transport layer that grows in importance as the HTTP MCP transport becomes the recommended integration path
- cli-e2e-tests is the sole zone that exercises cross-package behavior end-to-end; its zero-coupling guarantee means any regression in inter-package contracts (e.g. rex ↔ hench, sourcevision ↔ rex) will only surface here, not in unit or integration suites — preserve this zone's isolation strictly
- The architecture-policy.test.js guard is only effective if the e2e suite is run in CI — if the CI pipeline skips or gates e2e tests conditionally (e.g. only on main branch), structural violations can merge undetected. The policy test's value is entirely dependent on CI execution guarantees that are not enforced by the test itself.
- The 14-file suite list is truncated at 8; the 6 unlisted files are unknown. If any of those files import from application source (e.g. shared test fixtures that reach into package internals), the zero-coupling guarantee would be silently violated — the truncation makes this unverifiable from the zone metadata alone.
- The 6 files omitted from the zone listing cannot be audited for the zero-coupling invariant that gives this suite its architectural value. Confirm the unlisted files do not import from application source to preserve the black-box guarantee.
- architecture-policy.test.js is a naming outlier within the zone: all other visible test files carry a 'cli-' prefix that signals CLI command scope, but architecture-policy.test.js tests structural invariants, not a CLI command. Its zone membership is correct, but its filename breaks the implicit prefix convention shared by the other 13 files.
- All 14 e2e test files use the .js extension while every package in the monorepo uses .ts. This two-runtime configuration (transpiled TS packages + plain JS e2e tests) is an implicit convention that avoids a build step for e2e tests but is undocumented — a new contributor could write an e2e test in TypeScript and unknowingly introduce a required compilation step for the test suite.
- Rename architecture-policy.test.js to a name that does not imply CLI command scope (e.g. monorepo-policy.test.js or architecture-invariants.test.js) to preserve the 'cli-' prefix convention across the suite.
- Add a comment block or README in tests/e2e/ documenting that e2e tests are intentionally plain .js (no build step) so contributors do not convert them to .ts and inadvertently require transpilation in the test runner.
- [call graph] 367 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
